displayTitle: Four Methods, One Step That Decides Everything
kicker: PT LEVEL II // REMOVAL OF EXCESS

beats.narration: Every penetrant job runs the same seven-step skeleton. The four methods differ at one step — removal — and that's where sensitivity is won or lost.

payload.title: ONE STEP DECIDES
payload.sub: Four methods, one skeleton — they split only at removal.

beats.narration: Four letters name the methods by how excess comes off: A washes with water, B and D add a timed chemistry, C is hand-wiped with solvent.
Removal is the contested ground: it must strip everything off the surface yet take nothing out of the flaw. That tension defines each method's risk.
Postemulsifiable wins because removal is metered by time and concentration, protecting the flaw. So the highest-sensitivity fluorescent systems ride on Methods B and D.

chips.s: Penetrant washable inside the flaw too

chips.t: Method C rests on wipe discipline
chips.s: Solvent on the cloth, never the part

chips.t: B and D meter the chemistry
chips.s: Only the surface layer turns removable

chips.t: Classify it like a part number

beats.narration: So know where your flaw-trapped penetrant is protected, and you know the one control to guard. Removal decides sensitivity.

payload.sub: Find the protection — guard that control.
